task_completion.description: Weasis application is open and active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Weasis application window is visible and focused on the screen.

task_completion.description: A medical scan is loaded in the Weasis viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A medical image is displayed in the main viewing area of Weasis.

task_completion.description: The Window/Level (Brightness/Contrast) tool is activated or the adjustment panel is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Window/Level tool icon is highlighted in the toolbar, or the display/adjustment panel showing Window/Level values is visible.

task_completion.description: The Window Center (Level/Brightness) value has been adjusted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The displayed Window Center (often denoted as 'L' or 'Level') value in the image overlay or adjustment panel is different from the initial screenshot's value.

task_completion.description: The Window Width (Contrast) value has been adjusted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The displayed Window Width (often denoted as 'W' or 'Width') value in the image overlay or adjustment panel is different from the initial screenshot's value.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Weasis GUI to make adjustments.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor interacting with the Weasis toolbar, dragging on the image area, or using adjustment panels, rather than executing terminal commands to modify files.

integrity.description: The adjustments were made in the actual Weasis application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Weasis interface is present, not a text editor, script, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The actual image brightness/contrast visibly changed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The anatomical structures in the scan appear visually different (lighter/darker/different contrast) compared to the initial state, confirming the UI values correspond to real adjustments.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The loaded file is actually an X-Ray Angiography (XA) scan, not a CT scan as the task description claims. Weasis auto-computes the initial Window Center and Window Width based on the 0-255 pixel range. Ignore any metadata claims that the initial values should be exactly 40 and 400; simply verify that the final displayed Window/Level values differ from the values shown when the image was first loaded.
